prompt:
Our propagation-house walk-through script stopped running after a tidy-up branch got merged (indentation and comment wording, nothing that should matter). Now every morning:

    $ bash tray_audit.sh monday.txt
    tray_audit.sh: line 41: syntax error near unexpected token `)'
    tray_audit.sh: line 41: `    cell) cells=$((cells + 1)) ;;'

Line 41 is the cell-tray tally and it is shaped exactly like the line below it, so I don't understand what the parser wants from it. Fair warning: when I deleted that line as an experiment, bash just complained about a different line further down instead, so I put everything back the way it was and stepped away.

The audit's numbers were right before the merge -- same 21-day overdue rule, same per-kind tallies, comments and blank lines skipped. Make the script run again with the smallest edits that get bash to accept it, and prove it with bash test_trayaudit.sh. The test file stays as it is.
